text: Writing clean code is an essential skill for any programmer. The main goal is to make your code as readable as possible, so that both you and others can easily understand it. One way to achieve this is by using clear naming conventions: variables and functions should have names that describe their purpose, like `calculateTotalPrice()` instead of just `calc()`. Additionally, it helps to break your code into small, focused functions that each do one specific task. This makes the code more organized, easier to test, and simpler to modify later. Ultimately, clean code improves maintainability, saving time and effort in the long run.
